title: De-Coupling Planning from Execution
concept: Generating a complete plan upfront using a planner, then executing steps one by one using a worker.

exercises.prompt: Welcome to Plan-and-Execute Agents! Quick guess: what does an AI 'agent' do that a plain chatbot doesn't?

options.text: It takes actions and works toward a goal, not just chats
It only answers with one word
It refuses to do any tasks

exercises.explain: An agent doesn't just reply once. It pursues a goal by taking steps and using tools. In this unit, we'll see how smart agents split that work into planning first, then doing.

cards.title: Think before you leap
cards.body: Imagine cooking a new recipe. If you figure out every step before turning on the stove, you rarely burn dinner. AI agents can work the same way.

cards.title: Plan-and-Execute
cards.body: A planner writes out the full list of steps to reach a goal upfront. Then a worker carries out those steps one at a time. Planning and doing are kept separate.

cards.title: Architect and builder
cards.body: The planner is like an architect who draws the whole blueprint first. The worker is like the builder who follows that blueprint room by room.

cards.title: Baking with a plan
cards.body: Goal: bake a cake. Planner makes the list: 1) mix batter, 2) pour in pan, 3) bake, 4) frost. The worker then does step 1, then step 2, and so on.

cards.title: Not one big brain
cards.body: Myth: the agent decides everything all at once in a single thought. Truth: it first plans the whole route, then executes each step separately, checking progress along the way.

cards.title: Two jobs, one goal
cards.body: The planner thinks ahead; the worker gets things done. Separating them keeps the agent organized.

left.text: Writes the full step-by-step plan upfront

right.text: Executes each step one at a time

exercises.prompt: In a plan-and-execute agent, what is the planner's main job?

options.text: Create the complete list of steps before any action is taken
Do each step by hand as it goes
Chat casually with the user forever

exercises.explain: The planner's job is to look at the goal and produce the full plan upfront. The worker is the one that actually performs the steps afterward.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In plan-and-execute, the worker follows the plan one step at a time.
exercises.explain: Correct! After the planner writes the full plan, the worker executes those steps one by one to reach the goal.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about who makes the steps.
exercises.sentence: The planner creates the whole plan upfront, then the worker executes each step.
exercises.explain: The worker is the part that carries out the steps. The planner only designs the plan; the worker does the doing.

exercises.prompt: Which task list was written by an AI planner for the goal 'plan a birthday party'?

options.text: 1) Set date, 2) Send invites, 3) Order cake, 4) Decorate room, 5) Host party
party!!! idk just show up lol
cake maybe or not, we'll see how it goes

exercises.explain: An AI planner produces a clear, ordered, complete set of steps upfront. The vague, unordered notes are how a person might scribble, not a structured plan.

exercises.prompt: An agent must 'book a trip to the beach.' It first outputs: 1) check weather, 2) book hotel, 3) reserve car, 4) pack list. Only after that does it start doing step 1. Which statement best describes this?

options.text: It de-coupled planning from execution: the planner made the full plan upfront, then the worker executes step by step
It skipped planning entirely and just acted
The planner and worker are the exact same action happening at once
It only chatted and never made a plan

exercises.explain: This is the heart of plan-and-execute: a planner writes the complete route first, then a worker carries out each step in order. Separating the 'thinking' from the 'doing' keeps agents organized and easier to fix when a step fails.
